title: Palindrome String Check
description: Given a string `s` consisting of lowercase English letters, return `true` if `s` reads the same forwards and backwards (i.e. it is a palindrome), and `false` otherwise. Do not normalize the input; compare the characters as given.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters only
hints: Do opposite ends pointers — left aur right
Move left++ aur right-- jab tak match karein
Edge case: single char string is always palindrome

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
description: Given an array `amounts` sorted in non-decreasing order and an integer `target`, return `true` if there exist two elements at distinct indices whose sum equals `target`, and `false` otherwise.
constraints: amounts is sorted in non-decreasing order
hints: Sorted array hai to opposite-end pointers use kar
Sum chhota hai to left++ , bada hai to right--
Equal mil gaya to true

title: Reverse Array In Place
description: Given an array `cuisines`, reverse the order of its elements in place using O(1) extra space, then return the modified array.
hints: l=0, r=n-1 swap karte ja
Centre tak hi jana hai, warna double reverse ho jayega
Single element ka kuch karne ki zarurat nahi

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description: Given an array `skus` sorted in non-decreasing order, remove the duplicates in place so that each distinct value appears only once, keeping the relative order of the remaining elements. Return `k`, the number of distinct elements; the first `k` positions of `skus` must hold those distinct values. The contents beyond the first `k` positions do not matter.
constraints: skus is sorted in non-decreasing order
hints: Slow pointer 'write' index, fast pointer scan karta hai
Naya unique mile to slow ko aage badha aur copy kar
k = slow + 1 hota hai

title: Squares of a Sorted Array
description: Given an integer array `devs` sorted in non-decreasing order, return a new array containing the square of each element, sorted in non-decreasing order.
hints: Largest squares are at the two ends
Two pointers from both ends, fill result from back
Compare absolute values

title: Merge Sorted Array
description: Given two integer arrays `a` and `b`, each sorted in non-decreasing order, return a single array containing all elements of both arrays sorted in non-decreasing order. Duplicate values are kept.
hints: Do pointers i, j — chote wala uthao aur aage badho
Ek list khatam to dusri ki tail copy kar
Empty input ka edge case handle kar

title: Move Zeroes
description: Given an integer array `payments`, move all `0`s to the end while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements. Modify `payments` in place and return it.
hints: Slow = next write index for non-zero
Fast scan karta hai
Phir slow se end tak zero bhar

title: Is Subsequence
description: Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `s` is a subsequence of `t`, and `false` otherwise. A subsequence is formed by deleting zero or more characters from `t` without changing the relative order of the remaining characters.
hints: Same-direction do pointers — i for s, j for t
Match pe i++, hamesha j++
Empty s always true

title: Intersection of Two Arrays II
description: Given two integer arrays `a` and `b`, each sorted in non-decreasing order, return their intersection as a sorted array. Each value must appear in the result as many times as it appears in both `a` and `b` (the minimum of its two counts).
hints: Two pointers i, j
Equal mile to push aur dono badhao
Chota wala increment

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string `s`, return `true` if it is a palindrome when considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring letter case, and `false` otherwise. Non-alphanumeric characters are skipped; an empty cleaned string counts as a palindrome.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

hints: l, r pointers — non-alnum skip kar dono taraf
lowercase compare karna hai
Single char/empty cleaned palindrome hota hai

args: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given an integer array `heights` where `heights[i]` is the height of a vertical line at position `i`, choose two indices `i < j` that together with the x-axis form a container. The container holds `min(heights[i], heights[j]) * (j - i)` units. Return the maximum amount the container can hold.
hints: Greedy: chhota wala bar limit kar raha hai
Two pointer ends — chhota move kar
Always track max

title: 3Sum
description: Given an integer array `nums`, return all unique triplets `[a, b, c]` such that `a + b + c == 0`. Each triplet must be sorted in ascending order, and the returned list must contain no duplicate triplets, sorted lexicographically in ascending order.

examples.explanation: Two unique triplets sum to zero.

hints: Sort kar le pehle
Outer loop fix karo i, fir l = i+1, r = n-1
Duplicates skip karo dono levels pe

title: 3Sum Closest
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `target`, choose exactly three elements whose sum is closest to `target`. Return that sum. You may assume exactly one closest sum exists.
hints: Sort + fix one + two pointers
Track minimal diff
Update sum based on l/r movement

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array `tiers` containing only the integers `0`, `1`, and `2`, sort it in place so that all `0`s come first, followed by all `1`s, then all `2`s. Use a single pass and O(1) extra space, then return the array.
hints: Three pointers — low, mid, high
0 mile to swap low++ mid++
2 mile to swap high-- (mid same rakh)

title: Remove Element
description: Given an array `codes` and an integer `val`, remove all occurrences of `val` from `codes` in place and return the new length `k`. The relative order of the remaining elements must be preserved, and the first `k` positions of `codes` must hold them. The contents beyond the first `k` positions do not matter.
hints: Slow write index
Fast scan; non-val ho to copy
Return slow

title: String Compression
description: Given an array of characters `chars`, compress it in place: replace each maximal group of consecutive identical characters with that character, followed by the count as separate digit characters only when the count is greater than 1. Return the new length `k`; the first `k` elements of `chars` must hold the compressed result.
constraints: chars[i] is a printable ASCII character
hints: Read pointer, write pointer
Count by sliding read
Multi-digit count ko digit by digit append kar

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given a non-negative integer array `heights` representing an elevation map where each bar has width 1, return the total amount of water that can be trapped between the bars after raining.
hints: leftMax, rightMax track karo
Two pointers — chote side ka pani guarantee hai
Move the smaller side inward

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given a list `slots` of intervals where `slots[i] = [start, end]`, merge all overlapping intervals and return the merged intervals sorted by `start` in ascending order. Two intervals overlap when the end of one is greater than or equal to the start of the other.
hints: Sort by start
Then iterate maintaining current merged interval
Overlap to extend, warna push naya

title: K-diff Pairs in an Array
description: Given an array `nums` of distinct integers sorted in ascending order and a non-negative integer `k`, return the number of index pairs `(i, j)` with `i < j` such that `nums[j] - nums[i] == k`.
hints: Same-direction sliding pointers — slow & fast
Diff < k to fast++, diff > k to slow++
Equal mile to count++ aur dono badha

title: Longest Mountain in Array
description: Given an integer array `arr`, a mountain is a contiguous subarray of length at least 3 that strictly increases to a single peak and then strictly decreases. Return the length of the longest mountain in `arr`, or `0` if there is none.
hints: Find peaks: arr[i-1] < arr[i] > arr[i+1]
Then expand left aur right with two pointers
Track best length

title: 4Sum
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `target`, return all unique quadruplets `[a, b, c, d]` such that `a + b + c + d == target`. Each quadruplet must be sorted in ascending order, and the returned list must contain no duplicate quadruplets, sorted lexicographically in ascending order.
hints: Sort, fix two outer indices, then two pointer for inner pair
Skip duplicates at all four levels
Beware integer overflow — JS numbers safe under 2^53

title: Minimum Difference Between Two Arrays
description: Given two integer arrays `a` and `b`, each sorted in ascending order, find the pair `(x, y)` with `x` taken from `a` and `y` taken from `b` that minimizes `|x - y|`. Return that minimum absolute difference.

examples.explanation: Sort both; closest pair (11,8) has diff 3.

constraints: Inputs may not be pre-sorted; sort yourself
hints: Sort dono
Two pointers — chhota wala increment
Track min diff

title: 3Sum Smaller
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `target`, return the number of index triplets `(i, j, k)` with `i < j < k` such that `nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] < target`.
hints: Sort
Fix i, then two pointer l, r
Sum < target to (r-l) triplets count me add kar, l++

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Given an array `nums` of positive integers and an integer `target`, return the minimal length of a contiguous subarray whose sum is greater than or equal to `target`. If no such subarray exists, return `0`.
hints: Sliding window — two same-direction pointers
Right expand jab sum < target; left shrink jab sum >= target
Track min length

title: Maximum Trapped Water Between Two Pillars
description: Given a non-negative integer array `bars`, define for any pair of indices `i < j` the value `score(i, j) = sum over k from i+1 to j-1 of max(0, min(bars[i], bars[j]) - bars[k])`, i.e. the water trapped strictly between the two chosen pillars. Return the maximum `score(i, j)` over all valid pairs `i < j`.
hints: O(n^2) is acceptable here — fix i and j
Inner sum can be cumulative
Two pointers can prune by area upper bound — but n^2 brute force is fine for n<=1000
